pages.full_text:
[Photo of angel sculpture with vertical caption alongside: Keims XIII  Northern doorway.  Angel of the smile]

So' the second night I was cold & my hips aches. We played scrabble before bed with the heater on but the heater didn't work in the morning.
photo_captions: Keims XIII  Northern doorway.  Angel of the smile
pages.notes: The vertical caption alongside the photo reads 'Keims XIII  Northern doorway.  Angel of the smile' — likely referring to Reims Cathedral (Cathédrale Notre-Dame de Reims), France; 'Keims' is the diarist's spelling of Reims. The left page has no handwritten page number visible. The body text below the photo continues from the previous page's narrative.

pages.full_text: Monday  On to an [?Kuchen] [?Rofen] Supes - market way. Bes le luc. South east on motorways into Switzerland, towards Lucerne. We got off at Sursee & went down east side of Sempachersee Lake. Because it was so cold (nice + hot in day) at night we stayed in Sonne Seehotel (sun + sea hotel) on the lake at Eich. A room facing village was 220, but we faced lake (240€) which had a glorious view all evening + all night. Drank on terrace + lovely meal inside, veal liver (which weren't liver) + fish cooked in beer dough (batter?) Crème brulée. Colours v. restrained + masculine black + white + some gold – a gold wall – + thick ropes. Good quality. Children's playground stopped it being too formal. A workman/painter in splashed overalls came in for breakfast. Election round won by Macron (centrist) + Marine le Pen (fascist – will stop all immigration) Past Lucerne. Didn't see Bridge.  (No basin plug in hotel)  [?Margret] [?Citzegn] [?untermark]
pages.notes: Two photos glued at the bottom of the page: one showing an outdoor terrace/restaurant scene, the other a lakeside/waterside scene, neither with readable captions. The reference to 'Election round won by Macron (centrist) + Marine le Pen (fascist)' dates this entry to around the first round of the 2017 French presidential election (23 April 2017) or shortly after. The opening words after 'Monday' are difficult to read — possible readings include a place name or route note before 'Bes le luc' (possibly Besançon or Baume-les-Dames area). 'Bes le luc' could be Baume-les-Dames or similar. The final three words at bottom-right are very cramped and unclear: could read '[?Margret] [?Citzegn] [?untermark]' — possibly a note about Lucerne's covered bridge (Kapellbrücke) or nearby area. 'Sonne Seehotel' at Eich is on the Sempachersee in Canton Lucerne, Switzerland.
